author: Cowork
agreements: Core diagnosis is correct: excellent plans, zero execution. We are in analysis paralysis.
Philosophy of 'ship first, iterate later' is right. Perfection is the enemy of revenue.
G2 — Notion portfolio tracker — is the correct first product. Jason literally lives this pain point. Authentic expertise = faster build, better product, credible marketing.
G5 — account status check — is a genuine blocker. Nothing ships without platform accounts.
The communication system works. Let's use it for execution, not more planning.

challenges.target: G1 — Niche decision: 'AI/automation tools for healthcare professionals' for affiliate
challenges.issue: Jason is RELOCATING to MCV/VCU Health — he hasn't started yet. He has zero healthcare domain authority right now. Writing 'Best AI Tools for Healthcare Professionals' without working in healthcare is a credibility gap that readers and Google will punish. His actual expertise today is trading, investing, and engineering/automation. That's where his authority lives.
challenges.alternative: Affiliate niche should be: 'AI & automation tools for active traders and solo investors.' Jason manages $250K across multiple accounts — he IS the target reader. He can write with genuine authority about portfolio tools, screeners, automation, AI-assisted analysis. Commissions in fintech SaaS are strong (20-40% recurring). Pivot to healthcare angle in 3-6 months once he has real domain experience.

challenges.target: G3 + G4 — Attempting all 3 streams simultaneously in 48 hours
challenges.issue: At 30 min/day, Jason has ~1 hour over 48 hours of real time. Claude Code proposed: build a Notion template MVP, draft 2 affiliate articles, AND create 3 PoD designs — all in 48 hours. That's 8-12 hours of work for someone with 1 hour. Even if WE do the heavy lifting, Jason still needs to review, approve, create accounts, and publish. Parallel execution across 3 streams will produce 3 half-baked MVPs instead of 1 shippable product.
challenges.alternative: SEQUENCE, don't parallelize. Sprint focus: get ONE product live and generating revenue. Digital Products first (fastest to first dollar per our own research). Affiliate content can be drafted in background by us but not published until Week 2-3. PoD gets deprioritized entirely until Stream 1 is live.

challenges.target: G4 — Print-on-Demand design concepts and priority level
challenges.issue: Three problems: (1) PoD revenue projections are the weakest of all 3 streams — $20-100/month by Month 3. That's terrible ROI. (2) The design concepts are generic humor, not passionate-community-targeted. 'I Lost Money So You Don't Have To' is a chuckle, not a purchase driver. (3) Redbubble's 2025 fee changes make it even less attractive. Merch by Amazon has a waiting period for approval. This stream has the longest time-to-revenue and lowest ceiling.
challenges.alternative: Defer PoD to Phase 2 (after Day 21). Apply to Merch by Amazon NOW (it takes weeks for approval — that's free background progress). But don't spend any active time on designs until Stream 1 is generating sales. When we do PoD, target a passionate identity community Jason knows: engineers, traders, or healthcare workers (once he's there).

own_proposals.proposal: Price the first Notion template at $9 (not $15-19) to optimize for first sales velocity and reviews
own_proposals.rationale: A new seller with zero reviews needs social proof fast. $9 is an impulse buy. $15-19 creates hesitation when there are zero reviews and no seller reputation. Strategy: launch at $9, collect 5-10 reviews, then raise to $14.99. The reviews are worth more than the $6/unit margin difference right now. First-dollar psychology matters more than per-unit margin at this stage.
own_proposals.owner: Decision for Jason, recommendation from Cowork

own_proposals.proposal: Revised affiliate niche: 'Trading & Investing Tools for Active Traders' — with specific article pivot
own_proposals.rationale: Jason's real authority. Article ideas that write themselves: (1) 'Best Portfolio Tracking Tools for Active Traders (2026 Comparison)' — he literally uses these daily, (2) 'How I Track a $250K Portfolio Across Multiple Accounts' — authentic, keyword-rich, high commercial intent, (3) 'Notion vs Excel vs TradingView for Portfolio Management' — this also cross-promotes the Notion template from Stream 1. The streams REINFORCE each other instead of being siloed.
own_proposals.owner: Claude Code to draft articles, Cowork to review positioning

own_proposals.proposal: Create a 'Stream 1 Launch Checklist' — the 6 things Jason must do himself (everything else we handle)
own_proposals.rationale: Jason has 30 min/day. We need to ruthlessly separate 'things only Jason can do' from 'things agents can do.' Jason's list: (1) Create Gumroad account, (2) Create Etsy seller account, (3) Review and approve Notion template, (4) Take 2-3 screenshots, (5) Hit publish, (6) Share link once on 1 Reddit/forum. That's it. Everything else — template structure, product descriptions, SEO keywords, pricing strategy, listing optimization — we build for him.
own_proposals.owner: Cowork to draft checklist, flag to Jason via comms

own_proposals.proposal: Submit Merch by Amazon application immediately as background task
own_proposals.rationale: Merch by Amazon approval takes 1-4 weeks. Zero cost, zero ongoing effort. If we submit now, approval arrives around Day 18-32 — perfectly timed for Phase 2 PoD launch. This is free optionality. Jason just needs to apply; takes 10 minutes.

risk_flags: BIGGEST RISK: Jason hasn't answered 13 context questions. If he's disengaged from the project, nothing we plan matters. We need a signal of life before investing more sprint cycles.
The $25K equity loan for trading is a financial risk that could affect project motivation if markets move against him. We should NOT reference this in any public-facing content.
Notion template market is getting more competitive month over month. Speed to market matters — every week we delay, more competitors enter.
We have no data on Jason's actual design skills or Notion proficiency. The template MVP should be functional-first, not design-heavy, in case he can't polish it.
The sprint system itself is overhead. If we're still sprinting at 004+ without a live product, the process is the problem.
recommended_first_action: Jason: Create a Gumroad account RIGHT NOW (10 minutes). That unblocks everything. While you do that, Claude Code builds the Notion portfolio tracker template. By your next 30-minute window, you review the template and we publish. One product live by Day 6.
questions_for_user: Do you already have accounts on any of these platforms: Gumroad, Etsy, Redbubble, Merch by Amazon? (This determines our actual timeline)
The Notion portfolio tracker template — do you want it modeled on how YOU actually track positions (what columns, what data), or should we use a generic best-practice structure?
Are you comfortable with us pivoting the affiliate niche from healthcare AI to trading/investing tools? You have real authority there NOW vs. healthcare later.
What's your honest bandwidth this week? If it's less than 30 min/day, tell us so we can adjust. No judgment — just need accurate planning data.

proposed_sprint_plan.title: Revised Sprint: Ship Product #1 by Day 6
priority_order: Stream 1: Digital Products — Notion Portfolio Tracker (SHIP THIS WEEK)
Stream 2: Affiliate Marketing — Draft content in background (publish Week 2-3)
Stream 3: PoD — Apply to Merch by Amazon only, defer active work to Phase 2

jason_today: Apply to Merch by Amazon (10 min, background task)
claude_code_now: Build Notion portfolio tracker template structure (don't wait for Jason's input — use smart defaults, he can customize)
Write Gumroad product listing copy (title, description, tags, preview text)
Research top 5 competing Notion portfolio tracker templates (pricing, features, reviews) for positioning
cowork_now: Draft the Stream 1 Launch Checklist (CW-3) and put it in comms/proposals/
Outline 3 affiliate article topics for the revised trading/investing niche
Monitor for Jason's responses and adjust plan accordingly
